response: The director needs to arrange the scenes in a sequence where no two consecutive scenes come from movies released in the same decade using the release years 1994 2004 2007 2012 2015 and the decades {1990-1999} {2000-2009} {2010-2019} the scenes require 4 6 8 10 12 days respectively the optimal sequence that minimizes total shooting days is 1994 2004 2012 2007 2015 which corresponds to 4 6 10 8 12 days the total is 40 days the director wants to maximize the number of completed scenes within a budget of 50 lakhs the costs per day are 1 1.5 2 2.5 3 lakhs respectively the linear programming problem is to maximize the number of scenes subject to the total cost not exceeding 50 lakhs the maximum number of scenes that can be completed is 4 with a total cost of 40 lakhs ****** the director needs to arrange the scenes in a sequence where no two consecutive scenes come from movies released in the same decade using the release years 1994 2004 2007 2012 2015 and the decades {1990-1999} {2000-2009} {2010-2019} the optimal sequence that minimizes total shooting days is 1994 2007 2012 2004 2015 which corresponds to 4 8 10 6 12 days the total is 40 days the director wants to maximize the number of completed scenes within a budget of 50 lakhs the costs per day are 1 1.5 2 2.5 3 lakhs respectively the linear programming problem is to maximize the number of scenes subject to the total cost not exceeding 50 lakhs the maximum number of scenes that can be completed is 4 with a total cost of 40 lakhs
